---
title: "Git Notes"
format: html
---

## Basics of using git

Git is a distributed version control system (VCS) that is very popular.

The core steps to using git involve

1. Initiate a git repository
In RStudio we just click a little radio button on project creation
At the command line we type: 
`git init`

2. Do your normal work
Add files
Modify files. etc

3. Add your important files to the "stage"
3. Add your important files to the "stage"|In RStudio we click "staged" 
In RStudio we click "staged" radio button 
`git add yourfilename`


4. Commit your staged files to the repository 
In RStudio just hit the "commit" button and add git commit -m "your wee msg"

You can check your project "history" and status
'git_status"
'git log"


5. Git push to sync changes TO Github
The Up arrow in RStudio
`gitpush`

6. Git Pull to sync FROM Github

You can check you project "history and status
